justification: Wikipedia page for Demis Hassabis explicitly states he is a UK Government AI Adviser; the GOV.UK page found (gov.uk/government/news/world-leading-expert-demis-hassabis-to-advise-new-government-office-for-artificial-intelligence) confirms his advisory role to the UK government AI office, which preceded and fed into the Frontier AI Taskforce.
Federation of American Scientists expert page for Ben Reinhardt directly documents his affiliation as an expert at FAS.
Sayash Kapoor's Princeton CS homepage lists Arvind Narayanan as his PhD advisor; search snippets from Princeton Engineering confirm Narayanan advises Kapoor.
Marius Hobbhahn's post on X explicitly states he defended his PhD and thanks Philipp Hennig as his supervisor throughout the journey.
Dan Hendrycks's Berkeley CV lists his PhD advisors as Dawn Song and Jacob Steinhardt at UC Berkeley, directly evidencing the relationship.
Daniel Ho's Stanford about page lists his service as Senior Advisor on Responsible AI at the U.S. Department of Labor, naming both Ho and the DOL role explicitly.
Official FTC press release from Chair Lina Khan announcing Meredith Whittaker's appointment as Senior Advisor on AI, naming both parties in the described role.
Tim Wu's Wikipedia article states he studied under Lawrence Lessig at Harvard Law School, and search snippets confirm Lessig recognized Wu's talent and helped arrange clerkships for him.

Yejin Choi's Cornell dissertation 'Fine-grained Opinion Analysis: Structure-aware Approaches' credits Claire Cardie as her advisor who introduced her to NLP, directly evidencing the advisor relationship.
MIT DSpace repository page for Lynne Parker's dissertation 'Heterogeneous Multi-Robot Cooperation' supervised by Rodney Brooks, directly documenting the advisor relationship.
Arati Prabhakar's Wikipedia article explicitly states she served as Science and Technology Advisor to President Biden and OSTP director from October 2022 to January 2025, naming both parties.

Senate Commerce Committee press release on the Future of AI Innovation Act, which authorizes the U.S. AI Safety Institute at NIST, documenting the legislative relationship between the committee and NIST.

Official One-U Responsible AI Initiative page at the University of Utah documents the initiative's home in the SCI Institute, directly evidencing the affiliation between the Responsible AI Initiative and University of Utah.

Emory University news story about launching the Responsible AI website explicitly describes the enterprise-wide Responsible AI initiative led by the Emory AI Group with the Responsible AI Executive Governance Committee, naming both the initiative and Emory University.
Carnegie Mellon Block Center's Responsible AI page directly documents the Responsible AI Initiative housed within the Block Center for Technology and Society, naming both entities.
